prompt:
From the pottery studio's slack, #kiln-controller channel. The controller keeps 'forgetting' our firing profile between sessions and it's ruining loads. Saturday we dialed in the slow bisque — ramp 120, target 2232, hold 25 — hit save, fired, everything perfect. Sunday morning after the controller's nightly restart it ramped at the factory 150 again and the display said profile 'bisque-04' like we'd never touched it. No error on the panel, no error in the log. The profile file on disk is RIGHT — I can literally cat it and see ramp_rate 120 in the JSON. It saves fine. It just comes back from a restart acting like the factory profile.

Two more data points from this weekend. The quiet-hours window we set (21:00 to 05:30, so the horn doesn't wake the neighbors) also reverts to 22:00/06:00 after every restart, same silent way. And here's the kicker: if you fix one dial after a restart and save — say you re-set the hold time — the NEXT restart keeps your hold time but the ramp and target you'd saved before quietly revert to factory. It's like every save after a restart slowly shreds the profile.

While the controller stays up, everything behaves: tweaks apply, save writes the file, to_h shows the right numbers. Only restarts (save then load) misbehave.

The controller piece is FiringConfig in firing_config.rb, right here. Find it and fix it — small fix, no API changes, the on-disk file stays JSON. test_firing_config.rb is the acceptance contract; hands off the test file. Done when `ruby test_firing_config.rb` passes.
